Using the property of exponents that states $\frac{a^m}{a^n} = a^{m-n}$, we have
$$23^{11} \div 23^8 = 23^{11-8} = 23^3$$
Now we compute $23^3$.
$23^2 = 23 \times 23 = (20+3)(20+3) = 20^2 + 2(20)(3) + 3^2 = 400 + 120 + 9 = 529$.
$23^3 = 23 \times 23^2 = 23 \times 529$.
$23 \times 529 = 23 \times (500 + 29) = 23 \times 500 + 23 \times 29 = 11500 + 23 \times (30 - 1) = 11500 + 23 \times 30 - 23 \times 1 = 11500 + 690 - 23 = 11500 + 667 = 12167$.
Alternatively,
$23 \times 529 = 23 \times (500 + 29) = 23 \times 500 + 23 \times 29 = 11500 + 23 \times (30 - 1) = 11500 + 23 \times 30 - 23 = 11500 + 690 - 23 = 11500 + 667 = 12167$.

Another way to calculate $23 \times 529$ is:
$23 \times 529 = (20+3)(529) = 20 \times 529 + 3 \times 529 = 10580 + 3 \times (500 + 29) = 10580 + 1500 + 87 = 10580 + 1587 = 12167$.

Therefore, $23^{11} \div 23^8 = 23^3 = 12167$.
